blockReason: Visual QA stayed below 95/100 after 3 iterations (last score: 79). Defects: Imagery is very minimal and does not effectively attract attention or support the recruitment message.; The headline 'Bilfinger Shutdown Project' could be more prominent and visually emphasized to better capture immediate job clarity.; Contact and QR code CTA area is relatively small and low contrast against the dark background, reducing immediate visibility.; The use of space below interview dates is somewhat empty; this contributes to moderate underutilization of canvas space.; The brand logo at top-left is small and not emphasized, limiting brand presence.; Weak visual hierarchy, the main job title 'Bilfinger Shutdown Project' is prominent but the supporting country and sector info blends too much into the headline instead of standing out separately.; The top green bar with the agency name and registration number is visually cramped and lacks breathing space.; The interview dates boxes at the bottom feel disconnected and spaced unevenly, with the second Baroda date oddly shifted right, breaking the alignment balance.; The image lacks any photographic or graphic imagery that would increase attention and create relevance; it is purely text-based, which reduces the ad's power to stop users scrolling.; The overall layout is a bit dry and utilitarian; adding some subtle color highlights or separators to differentiate sections could improve scanning speed.; Some white space on the canvas is underutilized, particularly the center bottom area that might accommodate the interview dates better.; Imagery is very minimal and generic which reduces attention-stopping power; The headline does not dominate strongly enough over other text elements, reducing immediate clarity; Contact CTA is small and could be emphasized more for better visibility; Spacing between elements feels tight in some areas, affecting readability and scan-ability; The QR code looks somewhat stuck-in and lacks integrated design harmony with the rest of the ad

defects: Imagery is very minimal and does not effectively attract attention or support the recruitment message.
The headline 'Bilfinger Shutdown Project' could be more prominent and visually emphasized to better capture immediate job clarity.
Contact and QR code CTA area is relatively small and low contrast against the dark background, reducing immediate visibility.
The use of space below interview dates is somewhat empty; this contributes to moderate underutilization of canvas space.
The brand logo at top-left is small and not emphasized, limiting brand presence.

requiredCorrections.note: Use a more engaging and relevant background or imagery that visually supports the oil & gas shutdown project theme to increase attractiveness and commercial impact.
Make the job title and country more visually prominent to improve immediate clarity and stop potential candidates quickly.
Increase the prominence and contrast of the contact phone, email, and QR code area for better call-to-action visibility.
Better utilize space below interview dates to reduce dead zones and improve overall canvas utilization.

defects: Weak visual hierarchy, the main job title 'Bilfinger Shutdown Project' is prominent but the supporting country and sector info blends too much into the headline instead of standing out separately.
The top green bar with the agency name and registration number is visually cramped and lacks breathing space.
The interview dates boxes at the bottom feel disconnected and spaced unevenly, with the second Baroda date oddly shifted right, breaking the alignment balance.
The image lacks any photographic or graphic imagery that would increase attention and create relevance; it is purely text-based, which reduces the ad's power to stop users scrolling.
The overall layout is a bit dry and utilitarian; adding some subtle color highlights or separators to differentiate sections could improve scanning speed.
Some white space on the canvas is underutilized, particularly the center bottom area that might accommodate the interview dates better.

requiredCorrections.note: Add more padding in the top green bar around the agency name and registration to improve breathability.
Align the interview date boxes evenly in a consistent row or grid for balanced visual flow.
Incorporate a relevant, professional image or themed graphic to increase visual appeal and commercial impact.
Enhance the visual hierarchy by separating the country/sector line from headline text, perhaps by adding line spacing or color contrast.

defects: Imagery is very minimal and generic which reduces attention-stopping power
The headline does not dominate strongly enough over other text elements, reducing immediate clarity
Contact CTA is small and could be emphasized more for better visibility
Spacing between elements feels tight in some areas, affecting readability and scan-ability
The QR code looks somewhat stuck-in and lacks integrated design harmony with the rest of the ad

requiredCorrections.note: Use stronger, more relevant and attention-grabbing imagery related to shutdown projects or Gulf/Oil & Gas context.
Make the position and country headline visually dominant to improve immediate job and country clarity.
Add breathing space between position listings and interview venue sections to improve scan-ability.
Integrate the QR code design more seamlessly with the footer area; avoid looking like a sticker.
Enhance the visibility and prominence of the contact CTA details to increase user engagement.
